rationale: Round-7 AC runnability is sound. At pinned SHA 50165d81, both TestApproveImpl_WorktreeEnumRefusesDespiteProbeMiss and TestLifecycleOwnershipExactlyOneWorkflowClaimant have zero Go function hits, so their grep-first chains are genuinely RED; each pairs a function-existence discriminator with an exact-named go test -run. The former AC10 ownership shell one-liner is explicitly retained only as a non-discriminating regression pin and is indeed GREEN at the review SHA. Across AC1-AC13, every named test has an exact-named run; every new named test has its intended existence/anchor discriminator, while pre-existing AC2 happy-path tests are explicitly regression pins. AC11/AC12 proofs are unchanged. The deleted BranchExistsE/show-ref classifier negative pin remains present and correct, with both absent from pinned Go code. No dangling removed-test-name reference was found.

findings.detail: The new worktree-enumeration race test has grep-first existence discrimination, an exact-named run, and is absent/RED at 50165d81.
The new exactly-one-workflow-claimant test has grep-first existence discrimination and an exact-named run; the old already-GREEN shell check is explicitly demoted to a regression pin.
AC1-AC13 named tests have exact-named runs; negative classifier, AC11, and AC12 pins remain intact; no dangling test names were found.
